Review a Type Change

Type diffs often look harmless because they do not move runtime code. They still change the contract other code believes.

Before and after

// Before
type Invoice = {
  id: string;
  paidAt: string | null;
};

// After
type Invoice = {
  id: string;
  paidAt?: string;
};

The new shape has three states: present string, missing property, and possibly undefined. If callers used null as a meaningful state, the change needs a migration.

Checklist

  • What states did the old type allow?
  • What states does the new type allow?
  • Do serializers, API clients, and database rows agree?
  • Is there a test that covers the old edge case?
